_comment: Single source of truth for per-provider quickstart examples. Consumed by the SDK example scripts, the SDK parity test, and (via a vendored copy) the portal Quick Start page + its tests. impl=langchain is used only for providers whose LangChain chat class is mock-intercepted by the SDK (ChatOpenAI / ChatAnthropic) AND already shipped in traigent[recommended]; everything else uses LiteLLM, a core dependency that is universally mock-intercepted and needs no extra package. Keep ids stable: the FE selector and these example module names are keyed on them.

providers.label: Mock
providers.creds_note: Mock mode — no LLM provider key needed. LLM calls are intercepted and return canned responses.

providers.label: OpenAI
providers.creds_note: Set OPENAI_API_KEY to your OpenAI key.

providers.label: Anthropic
providers.creds_note: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to your Anthropic key.

providers.label: OpenRouter
providers.creds_note: Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY. OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible, so it reuses langchain-openai's ChatOpenAI with base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1.

providers.label: Nous Portal (Hermes)
providers.creds_note: Nous Portal is OpenAI-compatible but uses OAuth: it authenticates with a short-lived JWT minted from a refresh token, not a static API key. Set NOUS_API_KEY to a pre-minted token (returned as-is, no auto-refresh), or set NOUS_REFRESH_TOKEN / log in with the Hermes CLI (~/.hermes/auth.json) for auto-refresh via traigent.integrations.llms.nous_auth.get_nous_api_key(). It reuses langchain-openai's ChatOpenAI with base_url=https://inference-api.nousresearch.com/v1. Cost objectives need TRAIGENT_CUSTOM_MODEL_PRICING_JSON — the SDK ships no fabricated Nous prices.

providers.label: Azure OpenAI
providers.creds_note: Azure needs three values: AZURE_API_KEY, AZURE_API_BASE (your endpoint), and AZURE_API_VERSION. The model string is azure/<your-deployment-name> (the deployment name, not the base model). Via LiteLLM (core dep) because langchain's AzureChatOpenAI is not mock-intercepted by the SDK.

providers.label: Gemini
providers.creds_note: Set GEMINI_API_KEY (Google AI Studio key). Via LiteLLM (core dep) because langchain's ChatGoogleGenerativeAI is not mock-intercepted by the SDK; for Google Cloud Vertex AI use the GCP Vertex provider instead.

providers.label: Groq
providers.creds_note: Set GROQ_API_KEY. Via LiteLLM (core dep); langchain-groq is not bundled.

providers.label: AWS Bedrock
providers.creds_note: Bedrock needs AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) and a region (AWS_REGION_NAME). Model ids are region/account dependent and you must have requested model access. Via LiteLLM (core dep); langchain-aws is not bundled.

providers.label: GCP Vertex AI
providers.creds_note: Vertex AI needs a GCP project (VERTEXAI_PROJECT), a location (VERTEXAI_LOCATION), and Application Default Credentials — not just an API key. Point GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS at a service-account JSON or run `gcloud auth application-default login`. Via LiteLLM (core dep); langchain-google-vertexai is not bundled.

providers.label: LiteLLM (any provider)

env_vars.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
env_vars.placeholder: set the key for whichever provider(s) your models use

providers.creds_note: LiteLLM is a core Traigent dependency, so this works with no extra packages. Use provider-prefixed model strings (e.g. anthropic/..., groq/..., bedrock/...) and set the matching provider key(s). LiteLLM routes each call to the right provider.
